---
title: "*The Unauthorized Guide to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering* (Spreadsheet Techniques) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---

{{< include /LinkKey.qmd >}}

# Intro to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering

Spreadsheets are one of the most widely used computational tools in engineering practice. They allow engineers to organize data, perform calculations, explore parameter changes, and visualize results without writing full computer programs.

In chemical and thermal process engineering, spreadsheets are commonly used for:

- mass and energy balance calculations  
- parameter estimation and regression  
- uncertainty and error analysis  
- solving nonlinear equations  
- exploring the behavior of mathematical models  

The videos in this section introduce spreadsheet techniques that support these types of engineering calculations.

---

## The Unauthorized Guide to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering

This video introduces practical techniques for organizing engineering calculations in a spreadsheet. Topics include structuring calculations, separating input data from computed results, and designing spreadsheets that are easier to debug and maintain.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/5XMeQ6vWymA?si=CUrnajQV7rkgAmgv" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](/basics/calculations/BasicSpreadsheetTechniquesJTFVisuals.qmd)

---

## How to Have a Quickie with Your Data — Part 1

This video introduces spreadsheet techniques for **basic statistical analysis and data fitting**, including calculating the mean, standard deviation, and performing linear least-squares regression.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/4EfhlWNDQ48?si=Y4u8lUIBWluyVgtZ" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](/basics/calculations/Video25Visuals.qmd)

---

## How to Have a Quickie with Your Data — Part 2

This video extends the analysis techniques from Part 1 and demonstrates how spreadsheets can be used for **nonlinear regression and parameter estimation**.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/dVmh0EdQT1U?si=0A6c_LvtbfwbxQ7I" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](/basics/calculations/Video26Visuals.qmd)

---

## Solving Nonlinear Equations with Spreadsheets

This video demonstrates how spreadsheet tools such as **Goal Seek** and **iterative calculations** can be used to solve nonlinear equations numerically.

Such methods are frequently used in engineering calculations when analytical solutions are not available.

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/SaI2qdu1wSE?si=Gn3Q6EGuSdcoeUrU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allowfullscreen></iframe>

[Visuals](/basics/calculations/SolvingNonlinearEQsWithSpreadsheetsJTFVisuals.qmd)

---

# Examples and Definitions

## Example Links

- [Guide to Spreadsheets Template](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/GuideToSpreadsheets.ods)  
Spreadsheet containing the problem setup from the *Unauthorized Guide* video.

- [Guide to Spreadsheets Solutions](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/SolutionGuideToSpreadsheets.ods)  
Completed solution spreadsheet.

- [Data Fitting (Quickie) Template](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/DataFittingTemplate.ods)  
Spreadsheet template for the data-fitting examples.

- [Data Fitting (Quickie) Solutions](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/DataFittingSolution.ods)  
Completed spreadsheet solution.

- [Nonlinear Roots Demo](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/NonlinearRootDemoForPosting.ods)  
Spreadsheet demonstrating numerical root-finding.

---

# Definitions

Spreadsheet  
: A software tool that organizes data in a grid of rows and columns and allows calculations to be performed using formulas and functions.

Cell  
: The basic unit of a spreadsheet, identified by a column letter and row number (e.g., A1). A cell may contain data, text, or a formula.

Reference  
: A way of identifying a cell or group of cells used in a formula. References may be **relative**, **absolute**, or **mixed**.

Function  
: A predefined calculation built into the spreadsheet software. Examples include `SUM`, `AVERAGE`, `IF`, and lookup functions.

Drag-Fill  
: A spreadsheet feature that allows users to copy formulas or extend patterns by dragging the fill handle across adjacent cells.

---

# Other Links and Videos

## Previous and Following Videos

### Two videos back

**Interpol Is Late to a Mind-Blowing Math Hack, Just the Facts**  
*Linear Interpolation*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/VbnpWj9iYAU)  
[Info Page](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationWebPage.qmd)  
[Visuals](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationJTFVisuals.qmd)

---

### Previous video

**No Clear Interpretation a Mind-Blowing Math Hack**  
*Nonlinear Interpolation*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/721FxKwDRAU)  
[Info Page](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationWebPage.qmd)  
[Visuals](../../basics/calculations/NLInterpolationJTFVisuals.qmd)

---

### Next video

**How Deviant and Mean Are Your Data? Intro and Basics Part 1, Just the Facts**  
*Mean ($\mu,\ \bar{x}$) and Standard Deviation ($\sigma,\ S$)*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/ksg4Babav6o)  
[Info Page](../../basics/stats/BasicStatisticsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)  
[Visuals](../../basics/stats/5-JTF-WhyStatsJTF1Visuals.qmd)

---

### Two videos forward

**How Deviant and Mean Are Your Data? Part 2**  
*Standard Error ($S_{\bar{x}}$) and Confidence Interval ($2\lambda$)*  
[Video](https://youtu.be/cBOrE6eV-AE)  
[Info Page](../../basics/stats/BasicStatisticsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd)  
[Visuals](../../basics/stats/5A-JTF-WhyStatsJTF2Visuals.qmd)
